summary: A squat, multi-tiered rectangular building with rounded/stepped corners and horizontal banding. The walls are primarily wood planks with repeated stonebrick (stone brick) horizontal belt courses and corner/edge stonebrick details; the flat-ish roof has a raised stonebrick parapet with a dark red trim and glow blocks at corners. A low stonebrick fence/walkway surrounds the building on the ground. Interior is not visible; footprint appears mostly rectangular with chamfered/stepped corners.

shape.overall_form: rectangular block with chamfered/stepped corners and layered horizontal belts; slightly terraced tiers
shape.symmetry: approximate four-sided symmetry with mirrored long sides and repeated corner stepping

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: belt courses / corner detail / parapet / low fence

materials.name: redstone_block_or_terracotta
materials.role: thin dark-red trim under parapet (uncertain exact block)

elements: horizontal stonebrick belt courses around the perimeter at multiple heights
vertical stonebrick corner/edge pilasters stepping up the walls
wood plank wall infill with visible log/striped corner accents
flat roof with raised stonebrick parapet and dark red trim
low single-layer stonebrick block/walkway and spaced stonebrick slabs/blocks forming an outer border on ground
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint roughly 28x24 blocks then chamfer/step the four corners to create the rounded/stepped outline seen from above.
Stack wood plank walls with three evenly spaced horizontal stonebrick belt courses; add vertical stonebrick pilasters at the four corners and at the entrance projection.

Create a flat roof filled with a dark maroon material (nether brick appears used) and surround with a 1-block-high stonebrick parapet; place glowstone blocks on parapet corners and add a 1-block dark-red trim band under the parapet.
uncertainties: Exact block used for the dark red trim under the parapet is unclear (could be redstone block, red nether bricks, or red terracotta).
Ground border blocks (spaced blocks around perimeter) might be stonebrick slabs or full blocks; photos show slab-like heights but perspective leaves some ambiguity.
Interior layout/floors not visible; number of usable floors is estimated as one main story with decorative bands only.
